commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an oversplit-colon guard to Telegram target suffix parsing plus regression tests for malformed multi-colon targets and valid t.me topic suffix forms.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +71. Total +99 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main can split malformed multi-colon targets into chatId residue plus messageThreadId; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can tighten the t.me guard and add the missing regression tests without choosing a new product direction.

target.title: fix(telegram): prevent silent misparse of multi-colon delivery targets
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where `parseTelegramTarget` silently misparses malformed multi-colon Telegram delivery targets. A target like `chatId:topic:42:99` (4 colon-separated segments, e.g. from a typo or third-party plugin migration) is greedily split into `chatId: "chatId:topic:42"` and `messageThreadId: 99`, embedding `:topic:42` residue in the chat ID. This corrupts session keys (producing duplicate `:topic:` markers), breaks chat type resolution, and causes downstream Telegram API calls to fail with "chat not found".

The same bug class affects the explicit `:topic:` parser: `a:b:topic:42` silently becomes `chatId: "a:b"` with `messageThreadId: 42`.

## Why This Change Was Made

Both suffix parsers share the same chat-id/thread split invariant, so the fix applies a unified guard across both:

1. **Topic parser** (`^(.+?):topic:(\\d+)$`): Non-greedy `.` preserves URL-form targets like `t.me/mychannel:topic:9`. A shared `hasOversplitColons` guard rejects chatIds with unexpected colons that are not part of a known t.me URL form.
2. **Colon parser** (`^(.+):(\\d+)$`): Same guard — greedy `.` preserves `https://t.me/mychannel:9` while catching malformed inputs like `chatId:topic:42:99`.

Both guards are backed by new focused regression tests in `extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts`. No config, doc, or SDK surface changes.

## User Impact

- **Users with valid targets**: No impact. All supported formats (`chatId`, `chatId:topic:N`, `chatId:N`, `@username`, `t.me/...:N`, `t.me/...:topic:N`, `https://t.me/...:N`, `https://t.me/...:topic:N`) produce identical results before and after.
- **Users who accidentally produce malformed multi-colon targets**: The parser now falls through to an honest full-string fallback instead of silently producing a wrong chat ID. This prevents hard-to-debug session key drift and "chat not found" errors.

## Evidence

### Multi-colon misparse → full-string fallback (terminal output)

```
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  PR #97823 v3 — Topic + colon guard, t.me topic-form
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

── Multi-colon misparse → full-string fallback ──
  ✅ 1. chatId:topic:42:99 rejected
  ✅ 2. a:b:topic:42 rejected
  ✅ 3. deeply nested rejected

── t.me URL-form: legacy :N suffix ──
  ✅ 4. scheme-less t.me
  ✅ 5. full https URL

── t.me URL-form: explicit :topic:N suffix ──
  ✅ 6. scheme-less :topic:
  ✅ 7. https :topic:

── Regression: valid formats unchanged ──
  ✅ 8. plain numeric
  ✅ 9. @username
  ✅ 10. chatId:topic:N
  ✅ 11. chatId:N
  ✅ 12. prefix stripping

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
  12 passed, 0 failed
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
```

### Focused test suite

```bash
$ pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  46 passed (46)

$ npx oxlint extensions/telegram/src/targets.ts extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts
  Exit: 0
```

### Commit history

- `a9a74dc71b`: Unify oversplit guard across both suffix parsers (topic `. +?` + guard, colon guard inline)
- `937d09905f`: Add colon guard that preserves URL-form targets
- `e71f5bbaff`: Also guard `:topic:` parser against colon-bearing chatIds
- `74884f125e`: Prevent silent misparse of multi-colon delivery targets (initial fix)

### Diff stats

`extensions/telegram/src/targets.ts`: +27 -10  
`extensions/telegram/src/targets.test.ts`: +61 -0
